quotes.text: The question is raised, Why was Moses translated? President answered the question in this way: Our Heavenly Father desires that all mankind be led by the light, but that blessing will not be imposed upon anyone. Christ stands at the door and knocks; those who wish to have him enter and sup with them must hear his voice and "open the door" (Revelation 3:20). Thus two great principles on which the gospel is centered, love and agency, are plainly taught. Each of us is here to learn to love and give and hearken to the Spirit and choose to do the will of the Father. God wants his offspring and heirs to become all that we can be, to qualify for our inheritance. But we must choose; we are the decision makers, and he will not relieve that responsibility. As early as the book of Deuteronomy, it is written: "I have set before thee this day life and good, and death and evil; … therefore choose life, that both thou and thy seed may live: That thou mayest love the Lord thy God, and … obey his voice" (Deuteronomy 30:15, 19-20).
quotes.speaker: Marion D. Hanks
quotes.attr: Marion D. Hanks, in Conference Report, Oct. 1992, 85; or Ensign, Nov. 1992, 63

quotes.text: Therefore, if you did evil, you would die. Notice the statement of Paul, "the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is life eternal through Jesus Christ the Lord." That is Paul, restating Old Testament, putting Christ in it. Who did he call to witness against all the House of Israel that day? Heaven and earth. I have told you to chose life, and call upon heaven and earth to witness. If you ever do evil, you will receive death. King Mosiah, acting under the law of Moses, ritualized the execution penalty. He developed a ritual from these verses. Look at Alma 1, where Nehor is about to die an "ignominious death" because he killed Gideon while enforcing priestcraft.
quotes.speaker: Robert Norman
quotes.attr: Robert Norman, 1989 Old Testament Class Notes
